title: Rebuild of Evangelion confirmed for Super Robot Wars Link
display_title: Rebuild of Evangelion confirmed for Super Robot Wars Link
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild of Evangelion confirmed for Super Robot Wars Link             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Wed Aug 25, 2010 10:02 am http://superrobotwar.wordpress.com/2010/08/24/this-weeks-famitsu-magazine-60/ No word yet as to whether or not it's just 1.0 or if there will be more, but you guys with a DS can feel happy right about now. If it does do 2.0 maybe we'll finally know the names of Tunny and Clocky! Here are the other series: New series debuting: -Fight! Izer One -Izer-3 -Dancouga Nova -Macross Frontier -Linebarrels of Iron Returners: -Godannar -Steel God Jeeg -Gaiking: Legend of Daiku-Maryu -Mazinkaiser and the Mazinkaiser Movie -Gundam SEED Destiny Veterans: -Combattler V -Voltes V -Gundam SEED -Gundam Wing Endless Waltz No Getter Robo. Edit: If what I found at GameFAQs is true, than the L means Link.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Aug 25, 2010 10:12 am I have a PSP, not a DS, but this is interesting news. Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Wed Aug 25, 2010 2:51 pm I was excited, but then I remembered I suck at fighting games. :( We need an Evangelion Sim-City like game. Control Tokyo-3 and the Geofront, managing resources to beat the Angel invasion most efficiently.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Wed Aug 25, 2010 2:54 pm Gamer137 wrote: We need an Evangelion Sim-City like game. Control Tokyo-3 and the Geofront, managing resources to beat the Angel invasion most efficiently.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Wed Aug 25, 2010 2:59 pm Great Genius Shinji-Sama wrote: Gamer137 wrote: Invest mucho dinero in psychologist, guidance concilours, and other mentor figures for the pilots. Not to mention the rest of the staff.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Wed Aug 25, 2010 3:00 pm Gamer, Super Robot Wars games are in the strategy genre, not fighting. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Wed Aug 25, 2010 3:13 pm Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion wrote: Gamer, Super Robot Wars games are in the strategy genre, not fighting. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Wed Aug 25, 2010 3:43 pm Gamer137 wrote: Well my assumptions suck apparently. Worth looking into if I can ever find a way to get my DS to run it. To my knowledge the system is region locked.

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Wed Aug 25, 2010 4:04 pm Goddamn Gundam Wing. I'm tired of Wing Zero's useless ass. At least come with Hit+Away to pretend to be used.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Wed Aug 25, 2010 5:18 pm Looks like Shamshel's confirmed: I think I see the Graydon in one of Combattler V's pics and a Destroy Gundam in a SEED Destiny pic. So, anyone want to translate the story, please? Maybe the EVA stuff? 'Cause I see 2009 near Rebuild's stuff so it looks like 2.0 might be in after all.

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Wed Aug 25, 2010 5:23 pm Story: Human beings has advanced into space for a century. But it was not a peaceful path. Nuclear warfare has caused countless countries to reform, and the war against the Haniwa Empire (Jeeg) has caused a food shortage crisis on Earth. To solve this, the Earth has demanded supplies from the colonies, however that only resulted in a stale relationship between the two. And 15 years ago...the second impact has thrown the Earth into a chaotic state. And lurking in the backgrounds in this ruined Earth are unknown forces such as the Daris army (Gaiking), Kadou organization (Linebarrel) and Dancouga (WTF?!) has begin their operations...To counter this, the Earth has strengthened their forces and demand the colonies to help; thus further worsening their relationship. 5 years ago...human beings have won the war against the mimetic beasts, however the fighting for human beings have not ceased to disappear. 3 years ago, a terrorist attack on the colonies has put the people in space into a full fledged war against those on Earth. 2 years ago...The war around has lasted for over a year and the Earth's sphere side has won, but it has weakened all the countries' defense. The mechanic beasts army has waited for this moment and has now taken their move. But the Earth has united with the colonies and formed the united federation/alliance/whatever to stopped their invasion. The newly formed united federation/alliance/whatever succeeded in stopping the mechanical beast army and mikene empire from taking over the world. But this peace was shortly lived...after repelling the mikene empire, the Daris army has started to get serious. Not only that, the haniwa empire has revived to threaten the Earth's sphere once more. The only solution is to further strengthen their forces once more, but this has thrown humanity far away from the path of total pacificism. And now....contacts with visitors from another world has opened the curtains of a new war...

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Thu Aug 26, 2010 9:31 am Really awesome translation! Don't suppose you could do one for the Eva part of that pic, please? (maybe Combattler V for my inner fanboy :P) I find it a little strange that the Campbellians from Combattler V and Oz from Gundam Wing are nowhere to be mentioned although one could see the former as "alien visitors", although it is most likely referring to the Boazanians from Voltes V. Amazingly Combattler and Voltes are the only old school bots here. As for your questioning on Dancouga, the entire series is on YouTube, so I should see it sometime soon. I'm looking forward to that 'cause it's made by the same people that made Gravion.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Aug 26, 2010 2:08 pm Still no Gurren Lagann. I am disappoint.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Thu Aug 26, 2010 2:27 pm I think they're saving TTGL for their next console game. I imagine they will probably mix Heroic Age, Macross Frontier, Gunbuster and Diebuster, Shin Mazinger Z, Code Geass, and Gundam 00 in there as well. Shouldn't be too hard half those series need a time skip to properly do justice and two of them wouldn't do bad with that idea.

[]:             Quote Post by jojojoker » Sat Aug 28, 2010 8:45 am HELLS YEAH! I was pissed that SRW W on DS didn't have Eva but now I can't wait til SRW L although I really wish Nadesico was there too.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Aug 30, 2010 5:56 pm from Chara Hobby

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Mon Aug 30, 2010 6:00 pm Nice pic! Anything else you could give use TMBH?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Aug 30, 2010 6:05 pm Source article if you want http://game.watch.impress.co.jp/docs/news/20100830_390533.html

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Mon Aug 30, 2010 6:07 pm Darn it I can't understand Japanese. Anyone care to translate, please? Edit: http://www.gamefaqs.com/boards/605429-super-robot-taisen-l/56159501?page=2 If this article is true than Sachiel's confirmed as well. Of course, that shouldn't be surprising. What did get my attention is the fact that it said Combattler V was fighting a mimetic beast from Godannar. Either they're keeping the Campbellians a secret or they aren't in. Real shame, they're like my favorite old school robot villains, they were pretty cruel and heinous, they were pretty good villains. Also it seems to be that L stands for Link.

[]: Super Robot Wars L             Quote Post by CyborgZeta » Tue Aug 31, 2010 9:05 pm For those interested, Rebuild will be making its debut in the upcoming SRW game for the DS this November. You can watch the PV here: http://www.famitsu.com/news/201009/01032720.html
